Company configuration. Load via config.load() from N/config; returns a standard Record object.
Split into three separate objects: Script (deployment info, params), Session (session storage), User (role, dept, etc.)

methods.ss2Method: Depends on type: SCRIPT→Script.getParameter(), SESSION→Session.get(), FEATURE→runtime.isFeatureInEffect(), PERMISSION→User.getPermission()
methods.notes: Split across multiple methods based on setting type
Only SESSION type supported for writing

objects.notes: Build secure strings for HTTPS authentication

Uses hash options with algorithm param

objects.notes: CSV import task configuration
Entity deduplication task configuration

methods.notes: Merge is automatic; called at module level

objects.notes: SuiteScript error object. Also see error.UserEventError for user event-specific errors.

methods.notes: Property; renamed from code to name
Property; renamed from details to message

objects.notes: Split into two: serverWidget.Field for UI forms, record.Field for record metadata. Context determines which class.

methods.notes: Record field; returns array of {value, text}

Server-side vs client-side distinction is new in SS2. Use N/record for server scripts, N/currentRecord for client scripts.
Incoming HTTP request in Suitelet/RESTlet scripts
Split into ServerResponse (writing responses in Suitelets) and ClientResponse (reading HTTP call results)

methods.notes: Options object; accepts Form, List, or Assistant

objects.notes: Saved search object

methods.notes: Set title and id before calling save()

objects.notes: Search column definition. Created via search.createColumn(options).

objects.notes: Search filter definition. Created via search.createFilter(options).

objects.notes: Individual search result row
Set of search results. Key difference: SS2 uses iterator/range pattern.

methods.notes: Renamed; callback must return true to continue
Options object; max 1000 results per call

objects.notes: Select options are accessed via Field.getSelectOptions(), which returns an array of {value, text} objects
Sublist in a Suitelet form. Note: SubList renamed to Sublist (lowercase L) throughout SS2.

methods.notes: No batch set; use setSublistValue() in a loop

objects.notes: Subrecords return standard Record objects in SS2. No separate commit/cancel. Changes auto-commit when the parent record saves.

methods.notes: Subrecords auto-managed in SS2; no explicit cancel
Subrecords auto-commit when parent record.save() is called

objects.notes: Tab in a Suitelet form
Template renderer for generating PDFs and HTML

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: No SS2.1 equivalent for string replacement in SecureString
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Perform string replacement in plain text before creating SecureString, or use SecureString.replaceString() for partial support
No workaround; portlet refresh interval not supported

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: DateTime timezone handling moved to N/format module
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Use getCurrentSublistValue() then format.parse()/format.format() from N/format for timezone conversion

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: DateTime timezone handling moved to N/format module
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Use getValue() then format.parse()/format.format() from N/format

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: DateTime timezone handling moved to N/format module
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Use getSublistValue() then format.parse()/format.format() from N/format

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: DateTime timezone handling moved to N/format module
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Use format.format() to create formatted string, then setCurrentSublistValue()

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: DateTime timezone handling moved to N/format module
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Use format.format() to create formatted string, then setValue()

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: DateTime timezone handling moved to N/format module
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Use format.format() to create formatted string, then setSublistValue()

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: No equivalent in SS2 response objects
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Check ClientResponse.code for HTTP status codes; use try/catch for error handling
Use ServerResponse.setHeader({name: 'Content-Type', value: type}) and Content-Disposition header

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: UTF-8 is default in SS2; encoding rarely needs changing
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Use ServerResponse.setHeader() to set encoding if needed

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: No batch set method in SS2
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Use Sublist.setSublistValue() in a loop for each value

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: Subrecord paradigm fundamentally different in SS2
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Subrecords auto-managed in SS2.1. To discard changes, don't call parent record.save(). See SuiteScript 2.1 Scripting Subrecords.

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: Subrecord paradigm fundamentally different in SS2
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Subrecords auto-commit when parent record.save() is called. No explicit commit needed.

unmappedObjectMethods.reason: No equivalent in SS2 error objects
unmappedObjectMethods.workaround: Use error.UserEventError class for user event-specific errors, or check context in user event script
